intent: Add the repo-wide CI-mirror pre-push gate + requirements-sync dep check to testsAndMisc, so a push cannot go out green-locally but red-in-CI. Adapted for the python_pkg monorepo and the OOM-safe changed-packages runner (user's choice).
scope: Non-goal: no change to any python_pkg package or its tests.
changes: check_requirements_sync.py: AST-scans python_pkg for third-party top-level imports and fails if any distribution is absent from requirements.txt. Treats python_pkg's subpackages (imported bare, e.g. `from brother_printer import ...`) as first-party, and maps PIL->pillow / kasa->python-kasa / dateutil->python-dateutil / yaml->pyyaml.

ci_mirror.sh: pre-push gate = requirements.txt-only venv (hash-gated), `SKIP=pytest-coverage pre-commit run --all-files`, then the changed-packages runner in the clean venv (OOM-safe, no full raw pytest).
.pre-commit-config.yaml: added requirements-sync (pre-commit stage) and ci-mirror (pre-push stage) hooks; existing pytest-coverage hook left untouched.

verification.evidence: exit 0 after mapping PIL->pillow and treating brother_printer (a subpackage imported bare) as first-party; both were false positives, requirements already declared pillow.
run end-to-end (clean venv build + --all-files + changed-packages pytest) before committing.

risks: The clean-venv build fetches git deps; needs network. Hash-gated so it only rebuilds when requirements.txt changes.
check_requirements_sync only flags direct imports, not transitive; the clean-venv install in ci_mirror is the definitive backstop.
rollback: git revert the commit; remove the two hooks from .pre-commit-config.yaml. No package code is affected.
